description: Enrich learning extraction with full session conversation data by reading Claude's session JSONL files post-iteration, instead of relying on the sparse --print text output.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy` output
Rust: All tests pass with `cargo test`
No breaking changes to existing APIs unless explicitly required
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

mergeStrategy: Merge to main after MILESTONE-FINAL passes. Squash commits optional.

priorityPhilosophy.description: The hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN - Anticipate edge cases. Tests verify boundaries work correctly
2. FUNCTIONING CODE - Pragmatic, reliable code that works according to the plan
3. CORRECTNESS - Code compiles, type-checks, passes all tests deterministically
4. CODE QUALITY - Clean code, good patterns, no warnings
principles: Ship solid working code first, then use tests to improve it
Test the boundaries and exceptions - edge cases are where bugs hide
Handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production code

priorityGuidelines.medium: 21-50: Fix in current phase if time permits

acceptanceCriteria: Test: spawn_claude with session_id=Some(uuid) includes --session-id <uuid> in args
Test: spawn_claude with session_id=None does not include --session-id in args
Test: spawn_claude with no_session_persistence=true includes --no-session-persistence in args
Test: spawn_claude with no_session_persistence=false does not include --no-session-persistence
Test: ClaudeResult.session_id matches the passed-in session_id
Known-bad discriminator: test that --session-id is placed BEFORE -p (not after prompt text)
userStories.description: Write tests for the new spawn_claude parameters before implementation. Tests verify that --session-id <uuid> appears in args when session_id is Some, is absent when None, and that --no-session-persistence appears when requested.
edgeCases: session_id=Some with empty string — should still add --session-id (or should it be treated as None?)
Both session_id=Some AND no_session_persistence=true — conflicting flags, should warn or just pass both
session_id with non-UUID string — Claude CLI will reject, but spawn_claude shouldn't validate

failureModes.cause: --session-id placed after -p, becoming part of prompt text

invariants: --print and --dangerously-skip-permissions always present regardless of new params
userStories.notes: Follow existing test patterns in claude.rs (lines 500-1030) which use CLAUDE_BINARY=echo to capture constructed args. Tests may use #[ignore] until implementation lands.
userStories.title: Initial tests for spawn_claude session_id and no_session_persistence params

acceptanceCriteria: Test: valid session with text + tool_use blocks produces formatted output with [Tool: name] prefix
Test: thinking blocks are excluded from output
Test: queue-operation, progress, system, file-history-snapshot messages are skipped
Test: tool_use input truncated to 500 chars when longer
Test: tool_result content truncated to 1000 chars when longer
Test: global output capped at 50,000 chars
Test: missing session file returns None
Test: malformed JSONL lines are skipped, valid lines still processed
Test: session with only thinking blocks returns None (empty useful content)
Known-bad discriminator: test that tool_use input is JSON-serialized (not Display-formatted)
userStories.description: Write tests for the session file reader before implementation. Tests use inline JSONL fixtures to verify content extraction, truncation, and error handling.
edgeCases: Session file with only thinking blocks — should return None
Enormous tool result (>1000 chars) — truncated with ... suffix
Tool_use input with nested JSON objects — serialize to string then truncate
JSONL with trailing newlines or empty lines — skip gracefully
Unicode in tool results — truncation must not split mid-character

failureModes.expectedBehavior: Skip the line, continue processing remaining lines
Return None, log warning via eprintln!

invariants: Return type is Option<String> — None on any error, never panic
Per-block truncation: tool_use ≤ 500 chars, tool_result ≤ 1000 chars
userStories.notes: Use tempdir for session file fixtures. The reader function should accept a Path directly (for testability) rather than computing the path internally.
userStories.title: Initial tests for session JSONL reader

acceptanceCriteria: ClaudeResult has session_id: Option<String> field
spawn_claude accepts session_id: Option<String> and no_session_persistence: bool
When session_id is Some, --session-id <uuid> appears in args before -p
When no_session_persistence is true, --no-session-persistence appears in args
All 4 callers updated: engine.rs (session_id=None, no_session_persistence=false), ingestion/mod.rs (None, true), curate/mod.rs (None, true), curate/enrich.rs (None, true)
All existing tests pass (update test assertions for new arg count)
TEST-INIT-001 tests pass
userStories.description: Extend spawn_claude signature with session_id: Option<String> and no_session_persistence: bool. When session_id is Some, add --session-id <value> to args before -p. When no_session_persistence is true, add --no-session-persistence. Store session_id in ClaudeResult. Update all 4 callers to compile.
userStories.notes: Start by updating ClaudeResult and spawn_claude signature. Then update all callers with temporary None/false values so it compiles. Engine.rs caller will get real session_id in FEAT-003.

correctness: Session file reader must never crash the loop — all errors return None with a warning log
--session-id must be a valid UUID v4 (Claude CLI validates this)
performance: No performance impact — just adding args to command line
style: Match existing arg construction pattern in spawn_claude

userStories.title: Add session_id and no_session_persistence params to spawn_claude

acceptanceCriteria: New file src/loop_engine/session.rs created
pub mod session; added to src/loop_engine/mod.rs
read_session_for_learnings(session_id: &str, working_dir: &Path) -> Option<String> implemented
Path derivation: {HOME}/.claude/projects/{encoded_working_dir}/{session_id}.jsonl
Encoding: absolute path with / replaced by - (e.g., $HOME/foo → -home-chris-foo)
Assistant text blocks included as-is
Tool_use blocks formatted as [Tool: {name}] {json-serialized-input truncated to 500 chars}
Tool_result content truncated to 1000 chars
Thinking blocks, queue-operation, progress, system, file-history-snapshot skipped
Global output capped at 50,000 chars
Returns None with eprintln warning on missing file, parse error, or empty useful content
TEST-INIT-002 tests pass
userStories.description: Create src/loop_engine/session.rs with read_session_for_learnings(session_id, working_dir) -> Option<String>. Derives session file path from working_dir encoding + session_id. Parses JSONL line by line, extracts assistant text blocks and tool_use blocks (formatted as [Tool: name] truncated-input), tool_result content (truncated). Skips thinking, progress, system, queue-operation, file-history-snapshot. Applies per-block truncation (500 chars tool_use, 1000 chars tool_result) and global 50K cap. Returns None on any error with eprintln warning.
userStories.notes: Use std::env::var("HOME") for home dir (dirs crate not in deps). Design the core parsing function to accept a Path (for testability) with a thin wrapper that computes the path.

correctness: Per-block truncation must not split mid-character (use char boundary truncation)
Malformed JSONL lines must be skipped, not crash
Empty useful content (only thinking blocks) should return None
performance: Exit early if session file doesn't exist — don't attempt directory listing
Per-block truncation prevents one large block from consuming the 50K budget
Line-by-line JSONL parsing, not full file load
style: Follow existing loop_engine module patterns: public function, eprintln! for warnings
Match graceful degradation pattern from src/learnings/ingestion/mod.rs

userStories.title: Implement session JSONL reader

acceptanceCriteria: Engine generates uuid::Uuid::new_v4() before spawn_claude call
session_id passed as Some(uuid.to_string()) to spawn_claude
After iteration, read_session_for_learnings called with session_id and project_root
If session content available, used as learning extraction input instead of claude_output
If session read returns None, falls back to claude_output (existing behavior)
detection::analyze_output still uses claude_output (--print text), not session content
userStories.description: In engine.rs run_iteration: generate a UUID v4, pass it as session_id to spawn_claude. After iteration, call read_session_for_learnings and use the result (or fall back to claude_output) for learning extraction input.
userStories.notes: detection::analyze_output must continue to use --print text (claude_output) since it looks for specific markers. Only the learning extraction input changes.

correctness: detection::analyze_output must NOT be changed — it uses --print text markers
Fallback to claude_output must work when session read fails
performance: Session file reading is post-hoc, not on hot path
style: Follow existing engine.rs patterns for best-effort operations

userStories.title: Wire engine to generate session_id and read session for learning extraction

acceptanceCriteria: src/learnings/ingestion/mod.rs spawn_claude call passes no_session_persistence=true
src/commands/curate/mod.rs spawn_claude call passes no_session_persistence=true
src/commands/curate/enrich.rs spawn_claude call passes no_session_persistence=true

userStories.description: Update the 3 utility spawn callers (learning extraction, dedup curation, enrichment) to pass no_session_persistence=true. These already pass session_id=None from FEAT-001. Verify they no longer create session files.
userStories.notes: This was partially done in FEAT-001 (callers updated with false). Now change to true for utility callers only. Engine.rs caller keeps false.

correctness: Engine.rs loop iteration spawn keeps no_session_persistence=false

userStories.title: Add --no-session-persistence to utility spawns with real session_id=None
